rejected_simple_alternatives.name: CLI command printing text config tree
rejected_simple_alternatives.why_insufficient: Cannot show force-directed graphs, precedence waterfalls, or interactive path testing.

rejected_simple_alternatives.name: Static HTML export of config analysis
rejected_simple_alternatives.why_insufficient: No interactive graph, no subsystem filtering, no real-time path testing. Loses 'living map' value.

phases.title: Foundation -- Package, Types, and Walking Skeleton

steps.title: Config Explorer package with domain types
steps.description: Create @norbert/config-explorer package with discriminated unions for ConfigNode, ConfigEdge, ConfigScope, ConfigSubsystem, PrecedenceChain, and all supporting types.
acceptance_criteria: Package builds independently with zero @norbert/* dependencies
Discriminated unions cover all 5 scopes and 8 subsystems
All types are readonly (immutable domain)
Types exported from package index
architectural_constraints: Zero dependencies on @norbert/core or any other @norbert/* package
picomatch and gray-matter are sole runtime dependencies

steps.title: File classifier and JSON content parser
steps.description: Pure functions: classify file path to subsystem+scope, parse JSON settings files. Filesystem adapter in server reads files and passes to pure functions.
acceptance_criteria: File classifier maps known paths to correct subsystem and scope
JSON parser produces structured ParsedContent for settings.json
Malformed JSON produces error node, not exception
Filesystem adapter reads ~/.claude/ and .claude/ cross-platform
architectural_constraints: Classifier and parser are pure functions in config-explorer package
Filesystem I/O in server only (adapter boundary)

steps.title: Walking skeleton -- settings API and basic tree view
steps.description: End-to-end pipeline: read settings.json from user+project scopes, serve via GET /api/config/tree, render in minimal Svelte tree with scope coloring.
acceptance_criteria: API returns parsed settings from both scopes with scope annotations
Tree renders user settings in blue, project settings in green
Missing settings file produces placeholder, not error
Config tab appears in dashboard navigation
architectural_constraints: New Fastify route registered via registerConfigRoutes() in app.ts
Dashboard /config route with ConfigExplorer parent layout

phases.title: Config Parser -- All Formats and Subsystems

steps.title: Markdown and YAML frontmatter parser
steps.description: Parse Markdown files (CLAUDE.md, rules, skills, agents) using gray-matter. Extract frontmatter fields: paths, skills, tools, model, hooks, allowed-tools.
acceptance_criteria: YAML frontmatter extracted from rules, skills, and agents
Frontmatter fields annotated for display (e.g., 'Applies to: src/api/**/*.ts')
Files without frontmatter parsed as plain Markdown body
Malformed YAML produces error node, other files unaffected
architectural_constraints: gray-matter used for frontmatter extraction
Parser functions remain pure -- no filesystem access

steps.title: Full config discovery across all scopes and subsystems
steps.description: Extend filesystem adapter to scan all 5 scopes (user, project, local, plugin, managed) and classify all 8 subsystems. Assemble complete ConfigModel.
acceptance_criteria: All 5 scopes scanned with correct path resolution per platform
All 8 subsystems classified from discovered files
Plugin directories parsed including plugin.json manifest
Managed settings degrade gracefully when access denied
ConfigModel assembled with node counts matching filesystem
architectural_constraints: Cross-platform path resolution via os.homedir() and platform detection
Config file reader port injected into API routes

steps.title: Precedence resolver for all subsystems
steps.description: Pure function resolving precedence per subsystem following documented order. Handles override, additive (CLAUDE.md), and merge (array settings) resolution types.
acceptance_criteria: Settings precedence: managed > CLI > local > project > user
CLAUDE.md shown as additive (all ACTIVE, not overridden)
Array settings (permissions) show merge with source tagging
On-demand items labeled distinctly from always-loaded items
Override reason explains which scope caused the override
architectural_constraints: Precedence resolver is a pure function in config-explorer package
Resolution follows Research Finding 12 documented order

steps.title: Config Cascade view (precedence waterfall)
steps.description: Svelte component showing per-subsystem resolution waterfall with ACTIVE/OVERRIDDEN markers, strikethrough, override reasons, and subsystem selector.
acceptance_criteria: Cascade displays all 5 scope levels for selected subsystem
ACTIVE marker on winning value, OVERRIDDEN with strikethrough on suppressed
Subsystem selector covers all 7 subsystems
Click-through from cascade entry to Atlas file view
architectural_constraints: Data fetched via GET /api/config/precedence/:subsystem
Scope colors from shared CSS custom properties

phases.title: Atlas Tree and Path Rule Tester

steps.title: Config Atlas full anatomy view
steps.description: Dual-pane tree of ~/.claude/ and .claude/ with scope coloring, subsystem icons, content preview with syntax highlighting, and missing file indicators.
acceptance_criteria: Dual-pane tree shows user and project scopes side by side
Scope coloring consistent with Cascade and all other views
Content preview shows file contents with frontmatter annotations
Missing directories shown dimmed with descriptive tooltip
Malformed files display error badge, other files unaffected
architectural_constraints: Data fetched via GET /api/config/tree
Reuses existing dashboard shared components (EmptyState, StatusBadge)

steps.title: Path Rule Tester with glob matching
steps.description: Diagnostic tool: enter file path, see which rules match (with pattern) and which do not (with mismatch reason). Uses picomatch for glob matching.
acceptance_criteria: Text input accepts file path relative to project root
MATCH shown for rules whose glob pattern matches the path
NO MATCH shown with specific mismatch reason
Unconditional rules always shown as MATCH
Click-through to Atlas for rule file details
Glob matching uses picomatch-compatible semantics
architectural_constraints: Glob matching via picomatch in config-explorer package
API endpoint: GET /api/config/test-path?path=

steps.title: Config Mind Map overview visualization
steps.description: D3.js tree layout with 8 subsystem branches, element counts, scope coloring, expand/collapse interaction. Default overview for newcomers.
acceptance_criteria: 8 primary branches: Memory, Settings, Rules, Skills, Agents, Hooks, Plugins, MCP
Element counts per branch match Atlas file counts
Branches expand to show individual elements, collapse to counts
Empty subsystems shown dimmed with zero count
Scope coloring consistent with all other views
architectural_constraints: D3.js tree layout (existing library in dashboard)
Data from GET /api/config model

steps.title: Full-text configuration search
steps.description: Search across all config files with results showing file path, scope badge, subsystem icon, matching line. Cmd+K shortcut.
acceptance_criteria: Search covers all files across all scopes
Results show path, scope badge, subsystem icon, matching line
Click result navigates to file in Atlas
No-results state shows guidance message
Cmd+K (Ctrl+K) opens search from any view
Results return within 500ms for typical configs
architectural_constraints: API endpoint: GET /api/config/search?q=
Search index built from same scan as config model

steps.title: Cross-reference extraction and conflict detection
steps.description: Pure functions extracting relationship edges from parsed frontmatter (agent->skill, plugin->component, rule->path) and detecting naming conflicts.
acceptance_criteria: Agent skills: field produces agent-references-skill edges
Plugin manifest produces plugin-contains-component edges
Naming conflicts detected between non-namespaced elements at different scopes
Plugin skills show namespace prefix in node name
architectural_constraints: Cross-reference extractor is a pure function in config-explorer
Conflict detector operates on assembled node list

steps.title: Config Galaxy force-directed graph
steps.description: D3.js force simulation with typed nodes (shaped by type, colored by scope), relationship edges, subsystem filtering, plugin explosion, naming conflict indicators.
acceptance_criteria: Nodes shaped by type (hexagon=agent, circle=skill, etc.)
Nodes colored by scope consistently with other views
Edges represent actual cross-references from frontmatter
Subsystem filter hides non-matching node types
Plugin star expands to reveal contained components
Naming conflicts shown with red edge and tooltip
Graph renders within 2s for 100 nodes
architectural_constraints: D3.js force simulation (existing library)
Data from GET /api/config full model

quality_gates: Requirements traced to components (see architecture-design.md Section 12)
Component boundaries with clear responsibilities (see component-boundaries.md)
Technology choices in ADRs (ADR-009, ADR-010, ADR-011)
Quality attributes addressed (usability, maintainability, testability, performance)
Dependency-inversion compliance (config file reader port, pure parser functions)
Integration patterns specified (REST API, pure function calls, filesystem adapter)
OSS preference validated (picomatch MIT, gray-matter MIT)
